summary: This is a brief news roundup post where Joel Spolsky shares links about bootstrapping businesses without venture capital, promotes his book project collecting the best software essays of 2004, and advertises summer internships at Fog Creek Software. The post reinforces Joel's well-known skepticism of venture capital by linking to resources about avoiding VCs and bootstrapping. It also serves as a call to action for readers to participate in nominating essays and to refer talented interns.
key_points: Venture capital misaligns incentives between VCs and company founders
Bootstrapping a business without outside funding is increasingly viable
Joel was curating a collection of the best software essays of 2004
Fog Creek Software actively recruited smart CS undergrads and marketing students for internships
money_quotes: VCs do not have goals that are aligned with the goals of the company founders.
There's never been a better time to start a business with no money.
The pay is good, the benefits are great, and they'll get to make a real contribution to shipping software.
key_insight: Founders are better off bootstrapping than taking venture capital, because VC incentives are fundamentally misaligned with founder goals.
